tools.name: list_designs
tools.description: List designs available in this project (one per .sexp file in src/ containing a top-level design-block). Returns an array of objects with fields: name (file basename), title (human-readable design-block title), sections (array of top-level section names), instance_count, net_count, mtime (Unix seconds of last source modification), build_ok (false if the design failed to evaluate, in which case counts and sections are empty).

tools.name: read_design
tools.description: Return the full .sexp source text for a design. Agents should read, then optionally call write_design with an edited version.

tools.name: write_design
tools.description: Create or overwrite a design's .sexp with the full new source text. The previous version is auto-snapshotted before the write. Triggers a live rebuild and returns the new version + snapshot id.

source.description: Full S-expression source for the design

tools.name: edit_value
tools.description: Quick single-value tweak: change a component's value by reference designator (e.g. set C3 to 100nF). Same snapshot/rebuild semantics as write_design.

ref.description: Reference designator like C3, R1, U2

tools.name: edit_mpn
tools.description: Set MPN and/or manufacturer for a component in the .bom sidecar. Either field may be omitted; only the present ones are written. Does not touch the .sexp source — properties live in the .bom file. Bumps live_version so viewers refresh.

tools.name: edit_section
tools.description: Replace an entire (section "SECTION_NAME" ...) form with new_source. Use this when changing one section of the design instead of rewriting the whole file. new_source must include the outer (section ...) parens. Fails with AmbiguousMatch if the section name appears more than once. Snapshots and rebuilds like write_design.

section_name.description: Exact name of the section to replace, as in (section "Name" ...)

tools.name: replace_instance
tools.description: Replace an entire (instance "REF" ...) form by reference designator. Use this to add/remove pins, swap the component family, or change any single-instance detail without rewriting the surrounding section. new_source must include the outer (instance ...) parens. Snapshots and rebuilds.

ref.description: Reference designator of the instance to replace

tools.name: get_schematic
tools.description: Fetch the current scene graph JSON for a design. This is the same payload the browser viewer renders.

tools.name: get_version
tools.description: Return the current live version integer for a design. Increments on every mutation.

tools.name: run_checks
tools.description: Run ERC and/or DRC on a design. Optional filters: scope ("erc"|"drc"|"both", default "both"); severity ("error"|"warning"|"info", default all); changed_since (snapshot id from list_history — only ERC violations whose ref_des or net was added/removed since that snapshot are returned). DRC is not filtered by changed_since because its violations carry no structured ref. Returns {scope,filtered,changed_refs,changed_nets,erc:[...],drc:{violations,count}|null}.

changed_since.description: Snapshot id to diff the current design against; ERC violations are filtered to those touching instances or nets that differ.

tools.name: generate_review
tools.description: Build a structured design review document. Returns the same JSON shape as GET /api/review/:name: {design_name,title,generated_at,summary,sections[],power_budget[],bom[],assertions[],unresolved[]}. summary.status is pass|warn|fail. Rails in power_budget are sorted tightest first. unresolved is the flat list of error+warning ERC/DRC violations. Use this for a top-to-bottom audit before declaring a design done.

tools.name: list_history
tools.description: List snapshot ids for a design, newest first. Each id is a timestamp usable with restore_version.

tools.name: restore_version
tools.description: Restore a design to a prior snapshot. The current state is itself snapshotted first, so the restore is undoable.

tools.name: list_library
tools.description: List all names available in the project's lib/. Returns {components,modules,pinouts,footprints}, each entry with name + optional description. Use these names in (import ...) forms and instance definitions.

tools.name: read_library_file
tools.description: Return the .sexp source for a library file — use this to inspect a component's pin list, a module's parameters, or a footprint's pads before wiring them up.

kind.description: Which lib/ subdirectory to read from

tools.name: edit_note
tools.description: Replace the text of a single (note "...") form, located by a substring match on the note's current text. Returns AmbiguousMatch if the substring matches more than one note; use the full current text to disambiguate.

match.description: Substring of the note's current text used to locate it; must uniquely identify one note

new_text.description: Full replacement text for the note body (no surrounding quotes)

tools.name: add_import
tools.description: Add one item to the top-level (import ...) list. Dedup is word-boundary aware; returns DuplicateImport if the item already appears.

item.description: Library item name (atom-like; no whitespace, quotes, or parens)

tools.name: set_instance_pin
tools.description: Change one pin on an instance to a new net without rewriting the whole instance. Lighter-weight alternative to replace_instance for single-pin edits.

tools.name: list_instances
tools.description: List every instance in the design with its ref_des (auto-assigned), label (user-given name from source), component family, symbol, value, and pin count. Use this to bridge between source-side names (stm32, expansion) and board-side ref_des (U1, U8).

tools.name: list_free_pins
tools.description: List unassigned pins on an instance with their default pinout function names and a best-effort category (gpio|power|clock|analog|other). Use for picking GPIOs for new peripherals without scanning the full pinout file.

ref.description: Reference designator of the instance (e.g. U1)

tools.name: get_net
tools.description: Return every pin connection on a net plus any passive instances (R/L/C/F/D) on it. Use to audit decoupling or verify wiring without scanning the whole source.

tools.name: add_component_parameter
tools.description: Append a (parameter "NAME" TYPE) declaration to a library component file under lib/components/. Use to fix ERC "missing value" errors on user-uploaded components that lack a parameter slot. Snapshots the prior library file to history/_lib. Does not rebuild any design — re-run a build to pick up the change.

param_type.description: Parameter type atom (e.g. string, capacitance, resistance, number)

tools.name: add_component_requirements
tools.description: Append one or more full (requirement "text" ...) forms to a library component file under lib/components/. Each requirement is a complete s-expression so the body can include any mix of (ref "file.pdf" (page N) (quote "...")) and (check ...) clauses. Typical workflow: call read_library_file to see existing requirements, call list_datasheets and open the PDF in a browser at /datasheets/:filename to mine the datasheet for specs, then call this tool ONCE with every new rule you derived. Atomic: every form is validated and dedup-checked before any write — if any one is malformed or duplicates an existing rule (or another item in the same batch), nothing is written and the response identifies the offending index. Snapshots the prior library file once to history/_lib. Does not rebuild any design.

items.description: Full (requirement "text" ...) s-expression. Example: (requirement "VDD must be 2.4 V to 3.6 V" (ref "foo.pdf" (page 10) (quote "2.4V to 3.6V")) (check (voltage-range (pin "VDD") (min 2.4) (max 3.6))))

tools.name: list_datasheets
tools.description: List PDF files in lib/datasheets/ with size and the set of components that reference each (via (datasheet "file.pdf") in their .sexp). Use this to know what datasheets are available to cite from (requirement ...) (ref ...) forms.

tools.name: get_review_state
tools.description: Return the per-section review state: the checklist items (with id, text, checked) and per-section approval (approved, approved_by, approved_at). One entry per section slug present in the design. Use this to see which sections are still pending review.

tools.name: set_review_state
tools.description: Mutate one section's review state in one call: optionally append new checklist items, toggle existing ones by id, delete items by id, and/or set the approval flag + reviewer. At least one of add_items/toggle/delete/approved must be present. approved=true stamps approved_at with the current UTC time.

section_slug.description: Slug of the section to edit (match what generate_review returns)

add_items.description: Texts for new checklist items to append

toggle.description: Existing items to toggle by id
